label: Klaviyo
description: Connect Klaviyo to sync email and SMS marketing data, lists, segments, campaigns, and customer profiles.

form.title: Connect Klaviyo
form.subtitle: Paste your Klaviyo Private API key to give Anton access to your marketing data.

methods.label: Private API Key
methods.description: Paste your Klaviyo Private API key (starts with pk_…). The simplest and recommended path — no OAuth setup required.
methods.how_to:
## Generate a Klaviyo Private API Key

Klaviyo Private API keys grant server-side access to your account data. Do not use your Site ID (public key) — that is for client-side event tracking only.

### Steps

1. Log in to [Klaviyo](https://www.klaviyo.com) and click your account name in the bottom-left corner.
2. Go to **Settings → API Keys**.
3. Under **Private API Keys**, click **Create Private API Key**.
4. Give the key a name like `Anton` so you can identify it later.
5. Choose the access level — **Full Access** gives Anton the broadest read/write ability; use **Custom Key** to restrict to specific resources if you prefer.
6. Click **Create**. The key is shown once — copy it immediately.
7. Paste the key (starts with `pk_`) into the **Private API Key** field below.

### Notes

- The key is only displayed at creation time. If you lose it, generate a new one from the same **API Keys** page.
- To revoke access later, return to **Settings → API Keys** and delete the key named `Anton`.
- Never use your public Site ID here — it cannot authenticate server-side requests.

fields.name: private_api_key
fields.label: Private API Key
fields.description: Your Klaviyo Private API key starting with pk_. Found under Settings → API Keys. Do not use your public Site ID.
